summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Democracy / anti-fascism, AI / privacy. The strongest items in this run are FBI’s Kash Patel denies excess drinking amid officials’ US security concerns, Judge rules Trump administration violated the First Amendment in fight against ICE-tracking, and ICE deported 174 Daca recipients through most of last year, agency head says in letter.

mainItems.title: FBI’s Kash Patel denies excess drinking amid officials’ US security concerns
mainItems.summary: Agency director threatens to sue Atlantic for report citing allegations from two dozen current and former colleagues The FBI director, Kash Patel, is denying allegations detailed in a new report that he drinks to excess and has been…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Judge rules Trump administration violated the First Amendment in fight against ICE-tracking
mainItems.summary: Jorge L. Alonso, a federal district court judge for the Northern District of Illinois, said that the Trump Administration violated the First Amendment when it pressured Facebook and Apple to remove ICE-tracking groups and apps.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: ICE deported 174 Daca recipients through most of last year, agency head says in letter
mainItems.summary: So-called Dreamers – undocumented immigrants who arrived as children – were allowed to stay in US under Obama-era program From January through September 2025, US Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) deported 174 people that were…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: ‘We can’t wait’: Venice already seeking floods plan B five years after barriers’ launch
mainItems.summary: Rising sea levels and ecological damage caused by heavy use of flood defence system force city authorities to consider next move The Arsenale, the colossal shipyard that was the engine of the Venetian Republic’s domination for seven…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Help, there’s a cockroach in my coffee! 16 gross ingredients hidden in your favourite foods
mainItems.summary: From wood pulp in ice-cream to peat in portobellos, science has transformed how we dine.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Why are Harvard’s slavery researchers quitting or being fired?
mainItems.summary: The school’s $100m project to examine its slave ownership in Antigua is mired with controversy as academics allege obstruction Christopher Newman remembers seeing campus police officers as he walked into a human resources office at Harvard…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
mainItems.summary: The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It's dubbed the company a "RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY" full of "Leftwing nut jobs" and a menace to national security.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Three people sentenced to jail for bear-suit insurance scam in California
mainItems.summary: State investigated claims of bear attacks on cars, unconvincingly backed up by video of person in bear suit When it comes to the California department of insurance, don’t poke the bear.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Trump administration cleared to continue construction of White House ballroom, court rules
mainItems.summary: Construction was stopped after suit challenged president’s authority to raze East Wing without congressional approval The Trump administration can continue building a $400m White House ballroom at the site of the former East Wing, a US…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
mainItems.summary: Last month, OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool, and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Babies Born from Dead Parents Will Increase with New Tech. Are We Ready?
mainItems.summary: Reproductive technologies have enabled children to be posthumously conceived from the frozen eggs and sperm of deceased parents, raising legal, ethical, and practical questions.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
mainItems.summary: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: She invited her friends to come together to make her casket
mainItems.summary: Friends gathered at a weaver's studio in Massachusetts to help MaddyChristine Hope Brokopp make her casket.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
